product.name: Ghost Layer
product.tagline: Give your AI agent a body that vanishes — ephemeral execution with tamper-evident residue
product.description: Ghost Layer is a Python CLI tool and reverse-proxy library for running AI agents with scoped, time-limited credentials. Agents never hold standing keys — each session gets a fresh Ed25519 keypair, a TTL, and declared tool scopes. At evaporation, the private key is shredded and a tamper-evident signed action chain (residue) is left behind. Broker mode keeps real upstream credentials entirely out of the agent process.
product.the_problem: Today's agents execute with persistent, human-shaped credentials and unbounded scope. One leaked key compromises everything, and there's no signed proof of why the agent did what it did.
product.the_solution: Declare intent → spawn ephemeral session → execute scoped actions → evaporate (shred key) → leave Ed25519-signed residue. Change one byte and ghost replay reports verified: false.

cli_commands.description: Mint an ephemeral session + fresh Ed25519 keypair. TTL countdown begins. Args: --intent, --ttl, --scope (repeatable)
Bind an agent to the session via the intercept proxy. Args: --agent, --session-id, --port
Record a scoped, signed action. Blocked if tool is out-of-scope or session expired. Args: --tool, --action, --params, --session-id
Shred the private signing key, sign the whole action chain, finalize the residue. Args: --session-id
Re-verify every signature + the root chain signature. Args: --session-id or --all, --format csv
Start the GhostGateway reverse proxy. Sidecar or broker mode. Args: --upstream, --upstream-key (via env), --port

ghost_gateway.description: Reverse proxy / egress broker. Agents route all outbound calls through it.
ghost_gateway.on_evaporate: Gateway immediately rejects any further requests with the evaporated token

security_properties: Ephemeral keys: private signing key exists only for the session TTL, then shredded
Scope enforcement: agent can only call declared tools — out-of-scope calls refused before execution
Tamper-evident residue: Ed25519-signed action chain, verifiable after evaporation
Broker mode: real upstream credentials never reach the agent process
Upstream key: always read from GHOST_UPSTREAM_KEY env var, never a CLI argument

sdk_integration.description: Transport-injected — wraps any HTTP client or agent framework
sdk_integration.x402_example: examples/ contains an x402/XRPL payment agent settling a micropayment through a 60-second ghost body

product.ecosystem_role: Execution-safety layer beneath the x402 agentic web. Lets agents trigger payment rails (402Proof, NEXUS-402) and act on SqueezeOS signals without ever holding a standing key — every autonomous move leaves a signed receipt.
target_users: AI agent developers who need credential isolation
Teams deploying agents against third-party APIs

ecosystem.description: ScriptMasterLabs builds x402 micropayment infrastructure, AI agent trust systems, and institutional market intelligence tools. All products interoperate via the x402 protocol for agent-native payments.
